title: Those creepy little girls
wikitext:
[]: Those creepy little girls             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 16, 2006 4:42 am You know the part in 26' When Shinji builds a sand castle with two girls? Well, about them. THEIR EYES MAKE ME WANT TO SHOOT MYSELF IN THE FOOT. What the hell is with those creepy girls? Any idea?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Jul 16, 2006 5:06 am Looks just like porcelain dolls to me? (EDIT: or rather plastic) A wee bit too gnarly looking tho.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sun Jul 16, 2006 11:58 am That was freaky. I didn't really get that scene.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 16, 2006 12:12 pm Between the stage set, the lights, and the fact that the two girls are dolls could be seen as a manifestation of Shinji's belief that his life is constructed and controlled.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 16, 2006 12:27 pm Ornette made an interesting thread about the relevant sequence, here .

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 16, 2006 12:30 pm Are they both girls*? The woman is identified as Misato; the dolls' hair colours match those of Misato's two wards, her own little family of Eva pilots. * Cue the many Shinji-ko fan-arts.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 16, 2006 12:52 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Are they both girls*?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jul 16, 2006 12:54 pm Why does this thread make me want to do a parody of the "Barbie Girl" song from the 90's only with "I'm a Harpy girl in my Harpy world...." Complete with a harpy with a blond wig, pink dress, and pink high heels.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 16, 2006 2:47 pm I love little girls. They make me feel so good. I love little girls. They make me feel so ... bad. When they're around they make me feel like I'm the only guy in town. Oh... wait. Nevermind. </spam>

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Mon Jul 24, 2006 1:07 am they make me laugh

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Jul 24, 2006 1:17 am After reading some, the symbolism does make a little more sense now. But I still don't know why the little girls are those disturbing looking dolls. If they're supposed to be Asuka and Rei, wouldn't the scene make more sense if they were just plain Asuka and Rei as little girls? I think they were just looking for something they could do to make the scene seem disturbing, because otherwise, it'd just be kind of sad.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Mon Jul 24, 2006 7:40 am But if it's Rei as a little girl her neck should be sorta twisted and....never mind.

[]:             Quote Post by System Shocker » Mon Jul 24, 2006 2:13 pm Mako Chan.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Mon Jul 24, 2006 2:22 pm If it were really Rei and Asuka it would bring up the idea that Shinji might of known them as little girls- hence him knowing how they looked. Them looking as dolls but still appearing to be somewhat alike to their matured counterparts makes it seem how Shinji 'imagines' them.

[]: Repost from ANF (WARNING: VERY LONG)             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jul 24, 2006 3:31 pm (This was previously posted on the ANF thread that Reichu linked to) This was brought up in another thread and I think discussion of this scene needs its own thread. I don't know WTF is going on in this scene, and maybe people are going to need to think about the bigger picture a little more before analysing it, but I'll try to describe what's happening and some notes. Leading up to this, we have these few things: 1) Eva-01 merges with the spear, turns into the ToL 2) Fuyutsuki says this stuff: Having obtained them both, EVA-01 has become like a God. And now it has been restored to the propagule of souls... the Tree of Life. Will it become the Ark to save humankind from the nothingness of Third Impact? Or the demon that destroys us all? Our future lies in the hands of Ikari's son. (<-- is this important?) 3) A bunch of eyes pop open all over the surface of the ToL 4) GNK changes into GNR 5) Yui's voice: This Rei is your heart... Your very hopes and dreams... Rei's voice: What do you wish for? 6) Shinji's blissful face starts wavering (as if under water), we see boobs. The wave effect increases and it looks like there's light reflecting off of the waves (?). 7) The "drip" (usually indicating we're going from real-world, to imaginary-world or vice versa) The Sandbox Sequence starts immediately after the "drip". Everything's a little grainy, which is a first time we see this effect (correct me if I'm wrong). We already know this isn't happening in the real world, so why this graininess? There is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_lights.jpg]lighting equipment[/URL], as if on a "set" of a movie. We see a swing set, swinging back and forth and the music starts, the music is almost carnivalish pipe organ sounding. In the background, there are [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_sun.jpg]2 hills (kinda look like boobs) and a setting sun[/URL]. The sun here reminds me of a core for some reason, maybe because the hills looks like a female's chest and the sun is where the core would be. The dialog starts [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_playground.jpg]here[/url]. Shinji's voice: I see... It's just like when I started the cello. I thought that if I came here, I could find something... Then it cuts to a baby [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji.jpg]Shinji, staring at a mound of dirt[/url], there are 2 lights in the background on either side. It looks like he's wondering about something as he stares at the mound of dirt (which looks a lot like a pyramid that's been destroyed). Offscreen we hear: Come on, Shinji! , Let's build a castle! and instantly [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji2.jpg]his mood seems to brighten[/URL], he think's he's not alone anymore. He starts to pat the dirt (which definitely looks like a pyramid), then cut to the swing set, hills, and setting sun. Then we see the shot of the 2 other children. [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_child1.jpg]This one has short black hair[/URL] and the [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_child2.jpg]other one has red hair[/URL], both of them eerily looking like dolls. Dialog: Oh, here comes Mama! , We have to go home now! , Bye-bye! and the scene changes to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_mother.jpg]A mother (a striking resemblance to Misato) sitting on a folding chair[/url] amongst other scattered folding chairs on top of something that looks like a wooden stage. The other 2 children leave and Shinji is alone again. Cut to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_dirt.jpg]Shinji looking at dirt in his hands, he's motionless[/URL]. The other 2 children are now [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_children_mother.jpg]with their mother (Misato), hand in hand, and appears to be walking away[/URL]. You can hear the children saying "Mama" and laughing, in contrast to what's going on in the foreground, a lonely Shinji, umm, staring at dirt. Scene cuts to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_scaffolding.jpg]some scaffolding[/URL], you can hear a crow caw a few times. But notice on the edge of the left scaffolding we see [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_scaffolding_zoom.jpg]a figure standing there[/URL]. After a few seconds, [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_scaffolding_zoom2.gif]this figure leaves[/URL]. Is this Shinji, in an attempt to chase after his 2 friends but gets blocked by a void (the one separating the left and right scaffolding)?? Scene cuts to a [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_sadshinji.jpg]sad Shinji[/URL], his eyes watery and eyebrows trembling as if he's going to cry. Then his [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_sadshinji2.jpg]eye's open wide[/URL] as if he realizes something and his eyebrows stop trembling. We then see Shinji patting the sand again, even without his friends he decides to continue building the pyramid, you can hear a little sobbing from him. The cut changes to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_sun_shinji.jpg]the familiar swing, swinging back and forth, the boob hills, and the setting sun, with Shinji working on the pyramid in the foreground[/URL]. As he continues to work with the sand, a [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_streetlamp.jpg]street lamp comes on[/URL], probably indicating that it's getting "late". Finally, Shinji is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid.jpg]finished with the pyramid (Nerv)[/URL] and he stares at it for a while with an apparently emotionless guise. The background [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid2.jpg]dims considerably[/URL] while he still stares, this could be indicative of his mood change, from sad to angry, as the very next thing he does is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid3.jpg]lifts his leg up in preparation for a kick[/URL], and he looks pretty angry too. Now Shinji is kicking the sand pyramid, you see his foot connecting once, then the scene changes to the hills, setting sun, swing set[/URL] again, and in the background you can hear Shinji violently kicking the Nerv Pyramid he had built. The swing slowly stops swinging (it was swinging by itself this ENTIRE time, why does it stop??), and note that the street lamp (the one from the screenshot a few back) is turned on. We now see [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_destroyed_pyramid.jpg]Shinji's handiwork, a destroyed pyramid[/URL] (which looks a lot like the destroyed or half built pyramid from before Shinji started playing with the dirt). Crows cawing in the background here also. Cut to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji3.jpg]Shinji standing above the destroyed pyramid[/URL], 2 lights in the background and 2 hills (these lights look almost like eyes). Shinji seems to be contemplating what he had just done. A few seconds later, he [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji4.jpg]squats down and starts rebuilding the pyramid[/URL], sobbing the whole time, once in a while wiping the tears from his eyes with his sleeves. We now end this Sandbox Sequence with a zoom-out of [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_playground2.jpg]the playground, hills in the background, a sobbing Shinji rebuilding what he had destroyed, and the sun that has already set[/URL]. Before the scene changes, we hear Asuka saying "Jeeez! I get so pissed off whenever I look at you!". This leads RIGHT into the next quick sequence: an enraged Asuka, a passive Shinji (who appears to be underneath Asuka), a teary eyed baby Asuka crying out "Mama!", a sleeping Asuka whispering "ma... ma...", Shinji in Eva-01's cage, Shinji in the entry plug staring at Misato's cross saying "mama". Everything gets blurry now and off to the next sequence. This next sequence, which is ALSO grainy, is about Misato. This is where's she's having sex with Kaji, while Shinji and Asuka (and Misato) are observing and commenting. I'll stop here. A few observations: 1) Sandbox Sequence starts with Rei asking "What is it you wish for", then this sequence, then a lot of "mama". In this sequence, is Shinji sad about losing his friends or jealous that he doesn't have a "mama"? This all seems to me like it should be connected, but I can't think of anything atm. 2) This all seems to happen during the Pre-Instrumentality, the graininess is also happening during Misato's sex sequence. Could this mean that Shinji's playground sequence is also some flashback of some kind (with the children replaced with dolls and the "other" mother replaced with Misato)? 3) In Misato's sex flashback sequence (is it really a flashback? i.e. did it really happen the way it did in the sequence, sans Shinji of course), Misato, Shinji, and Asuka are actively observing and commenting on what's happening right before our eyes. In Shinji's sandbox sequence, there doesn't seem to be anyone observing and no comments being made, up until the very end when Asuka shouts "Jeeez! I get so pissed off whenever I look at you!". Could it be that Asuka was observing this pitiful display of Shinji this whole time? Or could it be that during this sequence, within Pre-Instrumentality, the specific souls that are to take place within Pre-Instrumentality are being drawn together as this sequence is being played out. 4) This whole Sandbox Sequence gives me the feeling that it's chock full of symbolism, much of which I'll either have to think more about or I'm just plain missing. What's the significance of the crow's caws? Desolation perhaps? 5) Are there any links to this in EoTV, particularly ep25? 6) What is the significance of the half built/half destroyed pyramid in the very beginning of this sequence? Seems like it starts with a half-build/half-destroyed pyramid, then he and children start rebuilding it, then Shinji (half) destroys it, then he starts rebuilding it again. There's been some discussion about the Sandbox Sequence in several other threads, here's some notable ones: [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=173345 starting at around post 49 [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=155082[/url] (crows caw) thewayneiac: "Crow calls are often used in Anime to question the characters' intelligence" Shin-seiki: "There are sound effects that go with certain situations in an almost clichÃ©d way; in NGE, crows cawing go with sunsets" [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=118872[/url] starting around post 70, how this sequence connects to EoTV ep25. From: [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showpost.php?p=5303651&postcount=77[/url] thewayneiac comments on this: "Shinji in a sandbox. The actual children from this long forgotten event are now doll like versions of Rei and Asuka. When they leave with their mother, who is portrayed by Misato, Shinji cannot go with them because he has seperated himself from the other characters." -------------------- Was thinking about what Shinji had meant when he said: "I see... It's just like when I started the cello. I thought that if I came here, I could find something..." A new interpretation: Could "I came here" mean when he came to Nerv? (thus the pyramid) This recount may not be exactly what's been happening in Tokyo-3, but more like what Shinji feels has been happening up to the point just prior to Pre-Instrumentality (remember the state of mind that he's in at this point). Shinji shows up at [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji.jpg]Nerv during the 3rd Angel attack[/URL] and Nerv HQ is being damaged (to some degree). He's a little angry about his Father calling him here just because he had a use for him, but soon he starts to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji2.jpg]open up a little[/URL]. He first meets [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_child1.jpg]Rei[/URL], then later he meets [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_child2.jpg]Asuka[/URL], both of which he considers his friends and co-workers (they're helping him (re)build the pyramid). [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_mother.jpg]Misato[/URL], who helps him open up a little, but who always seems a little emotionally distant to him, is also there. As he continues his stay in Tokyo 3, this illusion starts to fall apart, he feels as if his [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_children_mother.jpg]his friends and guardian start distancing themselves from him[/URL] and he feels [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_scaffolding.jpg]unable to follow them[/URL]. Shinji feels as if he's [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_sadshinji.jpg]alone again[/URL], but this time he's a lot more upset than before he shows up in Tokyo-3. Shinji then decides to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_sun_shinji.jpg]go to back to Nerv[/URL], but things don't get any better and [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid3.jpg]he loses it[/URL] (Let's say this is around the time after Kaworu's death and before the JSSDF invasion. The [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_swing.jpg]stopping of the swing[/URL] indicating the end of Shinji's recap of his stay in Tokyo-3). At this point, Shinji is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji3.jpg]completely useless[/URL], just a sack of meat being dragged around by Misato. But finally, he concedes and reluctantly [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji4.jpg]makes the decision to get back into Eva-01[/URL]. And that's where [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_playground2.jpg]it leaves him[/URL], completely alone, thinking that he has nothing. End Flashback. I know it's a bit of a stretch in a few places, but remember this is Shinji's recount of his time in Tokyo-3 in the broken state of mind that he's currently in. Also, I noticed all my screencaps got cropped a little on the right, my mplayer window must have been a little off the screen.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jul 24, 2006 5:36 pm The doll friends, like every other personal relationship in Shinji's life, are shallow and fleeting. Yet with their dull lifeless stares, Shinji can be secure in his isolation, and in control of the relationship. It's going to take a thesis to explain EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 24, 2006 10:02 pm "You don't explain EoE -- you fanwank it!"

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Jul 24, 2006 11:46 pm This is only sort of related to the topic, but what's the meaning of the one shot where Shinji appears to be having sex with Asuka, but has an "out of it" look on his face? It might make a little more sense if I watch EoE again, but I'm not really sure what it's supposed to represent. My best guess is that it's supposed to be an illustration showing Shinji feeling somewhat "impotent" in regards to his relationship with Asuka, because he was niether able to please her demands, or provide comfort when she needed it (which was largley Asuka's fault, since she rejected him when he tried, but Shinji might not see it that way). I was just wondering what other people's thoughts were.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Tue Jul 25, 2006 12:54 am Timstuff wrote: This is only sort of related to the topic, but what's the meaning of the one shot where Shinji appears to be having sex with Asuka, but has an "out of it" look on his face? It might make a little more sense if I watch EoE again, but I'm not really sure what it's supposed to represent. My best guess is that it's supposed to be an illustration showing Shinji feeling somewhat "impotent" in regards to his relationship with Asuka, because he was niether able to please her demands, or provide comfort when she needed it (which was largley Asuka's fault, since she rejected him when he tried, but Shinji might not see it that way). I was just wondering what other people's thoughts were.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 25, 2006 1:19 am Legend of the Past wrote: Whoa, that's a new one for me. At what part is it seen?

Is this Shinji, in an attempt to chase after his 2 friends but gets blocked by a void (the one separating the left and right scaffolding)?? Scene cuts to a [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_sadshinji.jpg]sad Shinji[/URL], his eyes watery and eyebrows trembling as if he's going to cry. Then his [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_sadshinji2.jpg]eye's open wide[/URL] as if he realizes something and his eyebrows stop trembling. We then see Shinji patting the sand again, even without his friends he decides to continue building the pyramid, you can hear a little sobbing from him. The cut changes to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_sun_shinji.jpg]the familiar swing, swinging back and forth, the boob hills, and the setting sun, with Shinji working on the pyramid in the foreground[/URL]. As he continues to work with the sand, a [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_streetlamp.jpg]street lamp comes on[/URL], probably indicating that it's getting "late". Finally, Shinji is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid.jpg]finished with the pyramid (Nerv)[/URL] and he stares at it for a while with an apparently emotionless guise. The background [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid2.jpg]dims considerably[/URL] while he still stares, this could be indicative of his mood change, from sad

Article: Those creepy little girls
 while with an apparently emotionless guise. The background [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid2.jpg]dims considerably[/URL] while he still stares, this could be indicative of his mood change, from sad to angry, as the very next thing he does is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid3.jpg]lifts his leg up in preparation for a kick[/URL], and he looks pretty angry too. Now Shinji is kicking the sand pyramid, you see his foot connecting once, then the scene changes to the hills, setting sun, swing set[/URL] again, and in the background you can hear Shinji violently kicking the Nerv Pyramid he had built. The swing slowly stops swinging (it was swinging by itself this ENTIRE time, why does it stop??), and note that the street lamp (the one from the screenshot a few back) is turned on. We now see [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_destroyed_pyramid.jpg]Shinji's handiwork, a destroyed pyramid[/URL] (which looks a lot like the destroyed or half built pyramid from before Shinji started playing with the dirt). Crows cawing in the background here also. Cut to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji3.jpg]Shinji standing above the destroyed pyramid[/URL], 2 lights in the background and 2 hills (these lights look almost like eyes). Shinji seems to be contemplating what he had just done. A few seconds later, he [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji4.jpg]squats down and starts rebuilding the pyramid[/URL], sobbing the whole time, once in a while wiping the tears from his eyes with his sleeves. We now end this Sandbox Sequence with a zoom-out of [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_playground2.jpg]the playground, hills in the background, a sobbing Shinji rebuilding what he had destroyed, and the sun that has already set[/URL]. Before the scene changes, we hear Asuka saying "Jeeez! I get so pissed off whenever I look at you!". This leads RIGHT into the next quick sequence: an enraged Asuka, a passive Shinji (who appears to be underneath Asuka), a teary eyed baby Asuka crying out "Mama!", a sleeping

Article: Those creepy little girls
 pissed off whenever I look at you!". This leads RIGHT into the next quick sequence: an enraged Asuka, a passive Shinji (who appears to be underneath Asuka), a teary eyed baby Asuka crying out "Mama!", a sleeping Asuka whispering "ma... ma...", Shinji in Eva-01's cage, Shinji in the entry plug staring at Misato's cross saying "mama". Everything gets blurry now and off to the next sequence. This next sequence, which is ALSO grainy, is about Misato. This is where's she's having sex with Kaji, while Shinji and Asuka (and Misato) are observing and commenting. I'll stop here. A few observations: 1) Sandbox Sequence starts with Rei asking "What is it you wish for", then this sequence, then a lot of "mama". In this sequence, is Shinji sad about losing his friends or jealous that he doesn't have a "mama"? This all seems to me like it should be connected, but I can't think of anything atm. 2) This all seems to happen during the Pre-Instrumentality, the graininess is also happening during Misato's sex sequence. Could this mean that Shinji's playground sequence is also some flashback of some kind (with the children replaced with dolls and the "other" mother replaced with Misato)? 3) In Misato's sex flashback sequence (is it really a flashback? i.e. did it really happen the way it did in the sequence, sans Shinji of course), Misato, Shinji, and Asuka are actively observing and commenting on what's happening right before our eyes. In Shinji's sandbox sequence, there doesn't seem to be anyone observing and no comments being made, up until the very end when Asuka shouts "Jeeez! I get so pissed off whenever I look at you!". Could it be that Asuka was observing this pitiful display of Shinji this whole time? Or could it be that during this sequence, within Pre-Instrumentality, the specific souls that are to take place within Pre-Instrumentality are being drawn together as this sequence is being played out. 4) This whole Sandbox Sequence gives me the feeling that it's chock full of symbolism, much of which I'll either have to think more about or I'm just plain missing. What's the significance of the crow's caws? Desolation perhaps? 5) Are there any links to this in

Article: Those creepy little girls
's chock full of symbolism, much of which I'll either have to think more about or I'm just plain missing. What's the significance of the crow's caws? Desolation perhaps? 5) Are there any links to this in EoTV, particularly ep25? 6) What is the significance of the half built/half destroyed pyramid in the very beginning of this sequence? Seems like it starts with a half-build/half-destroyed pyramid, then he and children start rebuilding it, then Shinji (half) destroys it, then he starts rebuilding it again. There's been some discussion about the Sandbox Sequence in several other threads, here's some notable ones: [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=173345 starting at around post 49 [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=155082[/url] (crows caw) thewayneiac: "Crow calls are often used in Anime to question the characters' intelligence" Shin-seiki: "There are sound effects that go with certain situations in an almost clichÃ©d way; in NGE, crows cawing go with sunsets" [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=118872[/url] starting around post 70, how this sequence connects to EoTV ep25. From: [url]http://www.animenation.net/forums/showpost.php?p=5303651&postcount=77[/url] thewayneiac comments on this: "Shinji in a sandbox. The actual children from this long forgotten event are now doll like versions of Rei and Asuka. When they leave with their mother, who is portrayed by Misato, Shinji cannot go with them because he has seperated himself from the other characters." -------------------- Was thinking about what Shinji had meant when he said: "I see... It's just like when I started the cello. I thought that if I came here, I could find something..." A new interpretation: Could "I came here" mean when he came to Nerv? (thus the pyramid) This recount may not be exactly what's been happening in Tokyo-3, but more like what Shinji feels has been happening up to the point just prior to Pre-Instrumentality (remember the state of mind that he's in at this point). Shinji shows up at [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji

Article: Those creepy little girls
 but more like what Shinji feels has been happening up to the point just prior to Pre-Instrumentality (remember the state of mind that he's in at this point). Shinji shows up at [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji.jpg]Nerv during the 3rd Angel attack[/URL] and Nerv HQ is being damaged (to some degree). He's a little angry about his Father calling him here just because he had a use for him, but soon he starts to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji2.jpg]open up a little[/URL]. He first meets [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_child1.jpg]Rei[/URL], then later he meets [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_child2.jpg]Asuka[/URL], both of which he considers his friends and co-workers (they're helping him (re)build the pyramid). [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_mother.jpg]Misato[/URL], who helps him open up a little, but who always seems a little emotionally distant to him, is also there. As he continues his stay in Tokyo 3, this illusion starts to fall apart, he feels as if his [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_children_mother.jpg]his friends and guardian start distancing themselves from him[/URL] and he feels [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_scaffolding.jpg]unable to follow them[/URL]. Shinji feels as if he's [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_sadshinji.jpg]alone again[/URL], but this time he's a lot more upset than before he shows up in Tokyo-3. Shinji then decides to [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_sun_shinji.jpg]go to back to Nerv[/URL], but things don't get any better and [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_pyramid3.jpg]he loses it[/URL] (Let's say this is around the time after Kaworu's death and before the JSSDF invasion. The [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_swing.jpg]stopping of the swing[/URL] indicating the

Article: Those creepy little girls
 say this is around the time after Kaworu's death and before the JSSDF invasion. The [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_hills_swing.jpg]stopping of the swing[/URL] indicating the end of Shinji's recap of his stay in Tokyo-3). At this point, Shinji is [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji3.jpg]completely useless[/URL], just a sack of meat being dragged around by Misato. But finally, he concedes and reluctantly [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_shinji4.jpg]makes the decision to get back into Eva-01[/URL]. And that's where [URL=http://buzzy.tesuji.org/images/sandbox_playground2.jpg]it leaves him[/URL], completely alone, thinking that he has nothing. End Flashback. I know it's a bit of a stretch in a few places, but remember this is Shinji's recount of his time in Tokyo-3 in the broken state of mind that he's currently in. Also, I noticed all my screencaps got cropped a little on the right, my mplayer window must have been a little off the screen.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Jul 24, 2006 5:36 pm The doll friends, like every other personal relationship in Shinji's life, are shallow and fleeting. Yet with their dull lifeless stares, Shinji can be secure in his isolation, and in control of the relationship. It's going to take a thesis to explain EoE.
